title: The Bullfrog
content:
He sat with no more compunction
    than an eel fish
    big-faced, bloated,
    the complexion of a beehive
     -    a dragnet of emotions
    crammed into a tumbler
    upended in water.

    His eyelids wore the effort
    of horseblinders, a
    spongy leather
    masquerading as torpedoes
    and I saw him
    lonely at the crossroads
    matted grass,
    a strip of wire, cold current
    chasing flecks about
    his person, then lunging green
    exploded into rapacity    - 
    caressed the awaiting fly strewn stick
    with emerald mouth &
    coffers of appetite.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Paul Cameron Brown delivers a powerful performance in "The Bullfrog"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
